methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Distalmotion is a Series G robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $150M+ to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 9 private robotics firms (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and one public comparable, though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier does not exist to support a reliable peer-median methodology.

Stage-progression analysis, applicable here, uses the $150M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement, yielding $188M. This method reflects the expectation that companies progressing through late-stage funding rounds command modest equity premiums relative to capital deployed. The $150M raised figure serves as the methodological anchor given the absence of a disclosed current valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $159M to $216M, with a median point of $188M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the lack of direct same-stage comparables—the broader robotics sector contains 11 peers but insufficient clustering at Series G—and reliance on a single applicable method. The valuation is materially dependent on the assumed step-up multiplier and the representativeness of prior fundraising rounds as a present-day value proxy. Investor diligence should prioritize revenue trajectory, unit economics, and market share dynamics to validate this range.
